response.justification: The candidate has a long, varied career with genuine developer-tooling credentials — including work on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack does not match. His experience centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack work, with only incidental Java, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no demonstrated deep JVM expertise, which are hard requirements for this role.
strengths: Prior IDE internals experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Built language tooling from scratch (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts for D)
Long track record across complex systems, low-latency services, and dev-ops infrastructure
concerns: No Kotlin and only marginal Java experience; no evidence of deep JVM knowledge
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise application-server background
Recent work is TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling, distant from the enterprise JVM ecosystem
